message:
🌱 Add k3s: Support cgroups v2 mission (#937)

Signed-off-by: github-actions[bot] <41898282+github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,79 @@
+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "k3s-900-support-cgroups-v2",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "k3s: Support cgroups v2",
+    "description": "Support cgroups v2. Requested by 10+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current k3s deployment",
+        "description": "Verify your k3s version and configuration:\n```bash\nk3s --version\nkubectl get nodes\n```\nThis feature requires a working k3s installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review k3s configuration",
+        "description": "Inspect the relevant k3s configuration:\n```bash\nk3s --version\nkubectl get nodes -o wide\nkubectl get pods -A\n```\n`k3s-agent` is failing to run on Fedora 31. I suspect this is due to cgroups v2 being the default on Fedora 31, but can't quite figure out how to see which version of runc is shipping with k3s (runc recently landed support for cgroups"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for Support cgroups v2",
+        "description": "This does not appear to be fully functional yet. On a Fedora 31 system, which uses Unified cgroups (full fledged cgroups v2), there is still a failure, though it does appear to get farther along in the process. Attached full\n```yaml\nOct 15 07:41:51 mercury systemd[1]: k3s-agent.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.\nOct 15 07:41:51 mercury systemd[1]: k3s-agent.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE\nOct 15 07:41:51 mercury k3s[23212]: time=\"2019-10-15T07:41:51.403568863-07:00\" level=fatal msg=\"failed to find memory cgroup, you may need to add \\\"cgroup_memory=1 cgroup_enable=memory\\\" to your linux cmdline (/boot/cmdline.txt on a Rasp>\nOct 15 07:41:51 mercury k3s[23212]: time=\"2019-10-15T07:41:51.403559607-07:00\" level=error msg=\"Failed to find memory cgroup, you may need to add \\\"cgroup_memory=1 cgro\n```"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected:\n```bash\nkubectl get nodes -o wide\nkubectl get pods -A\nkubectl get events -A --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10\n```\nConfirm the feature described in \"Support cgroups v2\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "This does not appear to be fully functional yet. On a Fedora 31 system, which uses Unified cgroups (full fledged cgroups v2), there is still a failure, though it does appear to get farther along in the process. Attached full log:\n[fedora31.log](https://github.com/k3s-io/k3s/files/5675856/fedora31.log).\nIt's a large file, so snippet included:\n```\n...",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "Oct 15 07:41:51 mercury systemd[1]: k3s-agent.service: Failed with result 'exit-code'.\nOct 15 07:41:51 mercury systemd[1]: k3s-agent.service: Main process exited, code=exited, status=1/FAILURE\nOct 15 07:41:51 mercury k3s[23212]: time=\"2019-10-15T07:41:51.403568863-07:00\" level=fatal msg=\"failed to find memory cgroup, you may need to add \\\"cgroup_memory=1 cgroup_enable=memory\\\" to your linux cmdline (/boot/cmdline.txt on a Rasp>\nOct 15 07:41:51 mercury k3s[23212]: time=\"2019-10-15T07:41:51.403559607-07:00\" level=error msg=\"Failed to find memory cgroup, you may need to add \\\"cgroup_memory=1 cgroup_enable=memory\\\" to your linux cmdline (/boot/cmdline.txt on a Rasp>\nOct 15 07:41:51 mercury k3s[23212]: time=\"2019-10-15T07:41:51.403539664-07:00\" level=warning msg=\"Failed to find cpuset cgroup, y"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
